'''Sam Wood''' (born 11 July 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Batley Bulldogs]], and [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and at [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League 1]].

== Background ==
Wood was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

He is a product of the [[Huddersfield Giants]] academy system.

== Playing career ==
=== Huddersfield Giants ===
On 12 February 2016, Wood made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 February 2016 |title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants 13-18 Wigan Warriors |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35535772 |access-date=17 January 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He played in a wide range of positions throughout his time with the Giants, including centre, wing, halfback, second row, and loose forward.<ref name="RLRKC" />

==== Dewsbury Rams (loan) ====
On 26 May 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=26 May 2021|accessdate=27 May 2021|title=Huddersfield Giants sextet set for Championship loan moves|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-loan-championship-rugby-20683895.amp}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 14 November 2021, it was announced that Wood had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=14 Nov 2021|accessdate=1 Jan 2022|title=Sam Wood explains thinking behind one-year Hull KR deal as he reveals Tony Smith plan|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-wood-hull-kr-deal-6199784}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 4 October 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Wood on a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 October 2023 |title=Sam Wood is a Tiger |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8783 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=4 October 2023 |title=Sam Wood: Castleford Tigers sign versatile back from Hull KR on three-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67009187 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He stated his intention to "put my imprint on the club and fight for that starting shirt," and added, "I leave everything on the field for the fans and I'll try my very best every week."<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=4 October 2023 |title='Put my imprint on the club': Sam Wood outlines goals after swapping Hull KR for Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/put-my-imprint-on-the-club-sam-wood-outlines-goals-after-swapping-hull-kr-for-castleford-tigers-4360006 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

Wood was assigned squad number 4 ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 2024 squad numbers: 11 new signings receive their jerseys, with 31 shirts handed out |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2024-squad-numbers-11-new-signings-receive-their-jerseys-with-31-shirts-handed-out |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against the [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 2 against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 February 2024 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-22 Castleford Tigers - Hosts hold on for first win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337686 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In December 2024, Wood was appointed as Castleford [[Captain (sports)|club captain]] by new head coach [[Danny McGuire]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=13 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Danny McGuire opts for rookie captain in fresh approach |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-danny-mcguire-opts-for-rookie-captain-in-fresh-approach-4909239 |access-date=13 December 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He scored his first try of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]] against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] in round 8,<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 April 2025 |title=Trinity stage late comeback to beat Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c367jedwp40o |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored a second at [[Magic Weekend]] two weeks later.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=4 May 2025 |title=Wakefield end Magic Weekend by beating Cas |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp8k0m84ny7o |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 13 October 2025, it was announced that he had joined [[York Knights]] for 2026.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=13 Oct 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Former Castleford Tigers centre Sam Wood joins York Knights|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-castleford-tigers-centre-sam-wood-joins-york-knights/}}</ref>

===International ===
He made his {{rlnt|England}} début on 29 June 2024 v {{rlnt|France}} in Toulouse.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=29 June 2024|title=France v England|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4465137}}</ref>
